question_title: Will Christie’s, Sotheby’s, or Phillips report a public auction sale of a painting for at least US$100 million before May 1, 2026?
summary: The question asks whether Christie's, Sotheby's, or Phillips will report a $100M+ public auction painting sale before May 1, 2026. A $236.4M Klimt sold at Sotheby's in November 2025, clearly qualifying under the title. However, the question was published Feb 25, 2026 (after the sale) and closes the same day. The CP at ~20% from 117 forecasters who can see the resolution criteria strongly suggests the criteria exclude prior sales and require a new sale in the Feb-April 2026 window. In that window, only London (March 4-6) and Hong Kong (March 29-30) sales are scheduled — no major NY marquee evening sales, which is where $100M+ results historically concentrate. The probability is anchored primarily on the informed CP signal and the auction calendar analysis.

factors.description: CP at ~20% from 117 forecasters who can see resolution criteria strongly implies criteria exclude the November 2025 Klimt sale, meaning the question is about new sales in the Feb-April 2026 window
$100M+ painting auction sales are concentrated in May and November New York marquee evening sales — the Feb-April window has no scheduled major NY evening sales for 2026
Spring 2026 auction calendar: London sales (March 4-6), Hong Kong sales (March 29-30) are the main opportunities, but these rarely produce $100M+ painting lots
Art market recovering strongly in 2025 (Klimt at $236M, Sotheby's up 17% YoY) — a strong market increases the small chance of a surprise $100M+ lot in an off-cycle sale
Residual probability that resolution criteria DO include the November 2025 Klimt sale (CP started volatile, question just published today)
Possibility of special unscheduled auction event (major estate, single-owner collection) producing a $100M+ painting before May 1

reasoning:
My forecast is **0.20 (20%)**.

**Key reasoning:**

1. **The Klimt sale probably doesn't count.** The $236.4M Klimt "Portrait of Elisabeth Lederer" sold at Sotheby's on November 18, 2025, which clearly satisfies the title. However, the question was published today (Feb 25, 2026), and the community prediction at ~20% from 117 forecasters (who can see the resolution criteria) strongly implies the criteria restrict the timeframe to exclude this prior sale.

2. **No major NY evening sales before May 1.** The marquee evening sales at Christie's and Sotheby's in New York — where virtually all $100M+ painting auction sales occur — are scheduled for mid-May, after the deadline.

3. **Limited opportunities in the window.** The Feb 25 – May 1 window only has London sales (March 4-6) and Hong Kong sales (March 29-30), which rarely produce $100M+ painting results.

4. **Small upside.** The recovering art market and small chance of a surprise special sale or the criteria actually including the Klimt sale provide modest upside probability.

resolution_reason: Gustav Klimt's "Portrait of Elisabeth Lederer" (1914-1916), an oil painting on canvas, sold at Sotheby's public auction on November 18, 2025 for $236.4 million. This meets all resolution criteria: (1) it was sold before May 1, 2026; (2) it was sold at one of the three specified auction houses (Sotheby's); (3) it is a painting; (4) the sale price of $236.4 million far exceeds the $100 million threshold; (5) it was a public auction sale, not a private sale. Sotheby's official website confirms this sale and the price, and multiple credible news sources (CNN, CNBC, ARTnews, The Art Newspaper, NBC News, Axios) corroborate the details. This was the most expensive work of modern art ever sold at auction.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if, before May 1, 2026, Christie’s, Sotheby’s, or Phillips publishes an official result or press release stating that a painting sold at public auction for at least US\$100,000,000. This question resolves to no otherwise. Primary sources are: [https://press.christies.com/section/press-releases](https://press.christies.com/section/press-releases), [https://www.sothebys.com/en/press](https://www.sothebys.com/en/press), and [https://www.phillips.com/press](https://www.phillips.com/press)  The question may also resolve based on [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions)
background_info:
This tracks whether the ultra-high end of the fine-art auction market produces another nine-figure painting result.
